title: Man'yoshu: Poems of a Myriad Leaves
description: The oldest Japanese poetry anthology: 4,500 poems from emperors, soldiers, farmers, and anonymous lovers.
introduction: The oldest Japanese poetry anthology: 4,500 poems from emperors, soldiers, farmers, and anonymous lovers.

translation.original_text: 大和は国のまほろば たたなづく青垣 山ごもれる 大和し美し
translation.text: Yamato is the finest of lands — green fences of hills rising in ranks: Yamato, beautiful in its mountain shelter.
